user_query:
I’m preparing for a coordination meeting about post–Hurricane Maria recovery in Puerto Rico. Please collect a small, verifiable set of official documents from FEMA, HUD, and the Puerto Rico Department of Housing (PRDOH) that relate to Puerto Rico’s recovery (e.g., program pages or PDFs that mention CDBG-DR, Public Assistance, Action Plans). Use the targets in input/targets.csv to guide what to search for and how to validate. Requirements:

1) Internet searching and discovery
- Build and run search engine queries (e.g., with site filters) to find at least one relevant, publicly accessible document per organization listed in input/targets.csv. Only use official domains that match each row’s domain_pattern (e.g., fema.gov, hud.gov, a pr.gov subdomain for PRDOH). Avoid non-official mirrors or third-party summaries.
- Save every exact query you used, one per line, to output/logs/queries.txt.
- Save the discovered document URLs (minimum 3 total, at least 1 per organization) to output/discovered_urls.txt, one URL per line. Do not hardcode URLs anywhere else.

2) Reproducible download and extraction
- Write and run a script (your choice of language) at scripts/fetch_and_extract.* that reads input/targets.csv and output/discovered_urls.txt, downloads each URL into output/data/raw/ (preserve original filenames where possible), and produces a structured metadata file at output/data/metadata.csv with columns:
  organization, domain, url, file_path, document_title, publication_year, file_type, md5, matched_keywords
  Notes:
  • document_title: use the HTML page title for web pages; for PDFs, use metadata/title if available, otherwise the filename.
  • publication_year: if a clear year is available in metadata or page content, put it; otherwise leave blank.
  • file_type: html or pdf (or other, if applicable).
  • md5: checksum of the downloaded file for reproducibility.
  • matched_keywords: semicolon-separated list of any keywords from the corresponding required_keywords in input/targets.csv that appear in the downloaded content text (case-insensitive). For HTML, parse visible text; for PDFs, extract text if feasible, otherwise leave matched_keywords blank.
- Capture the exact command(s) you ran to execute the script (including any arguments) into output/logs/run.log.

3) Meeting notes and action items
- Prepare output/reports/coordination_meeting_notes.md with three sections:
  • Overview: state the total number of documents collected and a per-organization count derived from output/data/metadata.csv.
  • Sources collected: one bullet per document in the form: “[ORG] TITLE — RELATIVE_FILE_PATH”.
  • Action items: 5–8 concise, practical bullets for follow-up. Each bullet must include an org tag ([FEMA], [HUD], or [PRDOH]) and reference at least one local file path from output/data/raw/ or output/data/metadata.csv.

Constraints
- Use the Internet to find and confirm the documents; do not rely on pre-known or hardcoded URLs.
- The script must operate using output/discovered_urls.txt as its only source of remote locations and must produce the artifacts under the specified output/ paths.
- Ensure each collected document’s domain matches the organization’s domain_pattern from input/targets.csv.

Deliverables to create under the workspace root
- output/logs/queries.txt
- output/discovered_urls.txt
- scripts/fetch_and_extract.* (and any supporting files it needs)
- output/data/raw/ (downloaded files)
- output/data/metadata.csv
- output/logs/run.log
- output/reports/coordination_meeting_notes.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
